summary: Adversarial impl-review of 1.10.0-RC3 presence before cut: 4 lenses + skeptic verify + synthesis

survived.title: Presence-event payload puts a raw (un-encoded) userId before pipe-delimited fields — a userId containing '|' silently loses the cross-node PRESENCE_CHANGE on every remote node
survived.scenario: firePresenceTransition builds the event body as `String payload = userId + "|" + t.getOldAggregate().name() + "|" + t.getNewAggregate().name();` (L1020). onPresenceMessage parses it with `String[] parts = payload.split("\\|", 3);` then `PresenceStatus.valueOf(parts[1])` / `valueOf(parts[2])` (L1040,1049). split(regex, 3) splits on the FIRST two pipes only. For a userId that legitimately contains '|' — e.g. a composite/authenticated principal like `tenant|alice`, or a SAML NameID — the payload `tenant|alice|ONLINE|OFFLINE` splits to ["tenant","alice","ONLINE|OFFLINE"]; length==3 passes the `parts.length != 3` guard, then `PresenceStatus.valueOf("alice")` throws IllegalArgumentException, which is caught at L1050-1052 and the event is silently DROPPED. Result: every REMOTE watcher node never receives that user's online/offline/away transition (incl. the crash-path reap OFFLINE), while the ORIGIN node's local-first listener fired correctly (it does not go through payload parsing) — so the loss is silent and asymmetric (works on the originating node, lost everywhere else). userId is taken verbatim from the handshake/principal (HandshakeUserIdResolver and any production UserIdResolver) with no '|' rejection anywhere, and '|' is a valid character in many principal formats.
survived.evidence: L1020: `String payload = userId + "|" + t.getOldAggregate().name() + "|" + t.getNewAggregate().name();`  L1040: `String[] parts = payload.split("\\|", 3);`  L1049: `l.onPresenceChange(parts[0], PresenceStatus.valueOf(parts[1]), PresenceStatus.valueOf(parts[2]));`. The Redis STORAGE path deliberately avoids this: RedisPresenceRegistry.b64()/key() base64url-encode the userId (RedisPresenceRegistry.java L238-249), and RC2's RedisUserRegistry.java L47/L217-218 explicitly documents base64url as 'delimiter-safe — no ":" / "|" / "{" / "}"' for exactly this reason. The RC3 event wire payload regresses that established convention by emitting a raw userId before two '|'-delimited fields. Fix: base64url-encode parts[0] on publish and decode on receive (or split from the right / use a leading length), mirroring the storage-side encoding. Note: the envelope codec itself is NOT the culprit — the userId rides inside the Base64'd envelope payload, so SimpleTextEnvelopeCodec's pipe-limitation does not corrupt it; the corruption is purely in this app-level `userId|old|new` body.
survived.why:
CONFIRMED as a real defect in the actual code; could not refute.

Verified the full behavioral chain in ClusterMessageSender.java:
- BUILD (L1020): `String payload = userId + "|" + t.getOldAggregate().name() + "|" + t.getNewAggregate().name();` — raw, un-encoded userId prepended before two pipe-delimited enum fields. Verbatim as cited.
- PARSE (L1040): `String[] parts = payload.split("\\|", 3);` then (L1049) `l.onPresenceChange(parts[0], PresenceStatus.valueOf(parts[1]), PresenceStatus.valueOf(parts[2]));`, wrapped in try/catch at L1050-1052 that only `log.warn`s and drops.
- Java semantics confirmed: for userId="tenant|alice", payload="tenant|alice|ONLINE|OFFLINE"; split("\\|", 3) → ["tenant","alice","ONLINE|OFFLINE"]; length==3 PASSES the `parts.length != 3` guard (L1041); then `PresenceStatus.valueOf("alice")` throws IllegalArgumentException → caught → event silently dropped on the remote node.
- ASYMMETRY confirmed: the origin node fires the local listener at L1010-1018 directly with real enum objects (t.getOldAggregate()/t.getNewAggregate()), never going through string parsing, so it works; only REMOTE nodes lose the event. This includes the crash-path reap OFFLINE (reapPresenceForDeadNode → firePresenceTransition, L1073), so the authoritative OFFLINE correction is also lost on remote watchers.

userId can legitimately contain '|': HandshakeUserIdResolver.resolve (L97-104) returns the handshake query-param/header value verbatim (only .trim()); the UserIdResolver SPI contract (javadoc L37-55) requires only "stable across reconnects" + "distinct per user" with NO character restriction. A Grep for any userId '|'-rejection/validation guard across the whole module returned no matches — none exists. Composite/multi-tenant principals (tenant|alice), SAML NameIDs, and LDAP DNs routinely contain '|'.

The project already treats '|'-bearing userIds as a known hazard: RedisPresenceRegistry.b64()/key() (L238-249) base64url-encode the userId for storage, and RedisUserRegistry's javadoc (L46-48) explicitly documents base64url as "delimiter-safe — no ':' / '|' / '{' / '}'" for exactly this reason. The RC3 event wire payload regresses that established convention.

Adversarial counter-arguments rejected: (1) "spec mandates userId|old|new so it's by-design" — the spec (§6 L152) does prescribe that raw format, but the same spec guarantees (PresenceChangeListener javadoc L59-60, §6) the event fires "on every RC3 node"; a '|'-bearing userId silently breaks that guarantee on every remote node, so the spec's payload format is the source of the realized defect, not an exoneration (the finding correctly proposes base64url-encoding parts[0], mirroring storage). (2) "exotic" — the default resolver trusts client input verbatim and the project's own storage layer defends against this exact character, so it is not dismissible. (3) "it logs, not silent" — it warns on the remote node only; the application's PresenceChangeListener never fires on any remote node, so from the app/roster perspective the transition is silently lost.

No test exercises a '|'-containing userId across the parse path (ClusterPresenceTest uses simple userIds), so the gap is untested as well.

Severity MAJOR is correctly calibrated: it is a cross-node correctness/silent-loss defect that defeats the core RC3 deliverable (including the crash-path OFFLINE event) for affected users, but it is conditional on a '|' appearing in the userId rather than affecting all traffic, so it is below BLOCKER.

backlog.title: Presence-event wire payload emits a raw un-encoded userId before two '|'-delimited enum fields; a userId containing '|' silently drops the cross-node PRESENCE_CHANGE on every remote node (including crash-path reap OFFLINE)
backlog.rationale: Verified real in the actual code: build at L1020 (userId + "|" + old + "|" + new), parse at L1040 split("\\|",3) + L1049 PresenceStatus.valueOf(parts[1]/parts[2]) inside a try/catch (L1050-1052) that only log.warns and drops. For userId='tenant|alice' the payload splits to ["tenant","alice","ONLINE|OFFLINE"], passes the parts.length!=3 guard, then valueOf("alice") throws and the event is silently dropped on every REMOTE node. Origin node is unaffected (L1013 fires the listener with enum objects, no parsing), so the loss is asymmetric and silent; the crash-path reap OFFLINE (reapPresenceForDeadNode L1062-1076 -> firePresenceTransition L1073) is among the events lost on remote watchers. Default HandshakeUserIdResolver returns the principal verbatim with no '|' rejection; the project's own storage layer (RedisPresenceRegistry.b64()/key() L238-249, RedisUserRegistry javadoc L46-48) already base64url-encodes userId precisely because '|' is a delimiter hazard, so the event wire format regresses an established convention. Does NOT gate the cut: the reap machinery, reserved PRESENCE_CHANNEL + no-version-bump, OnAnyRedisSpiRequired gating, and the byte-identical-when-off contract (whole path gated behind presencePublishChanges / presence beans) are all intact; defect is conditional on '|' in userId, not all traffic.
backlog.oneLineFix: Base64url-encode parts[0] on publish (L1020) and decode on receive (L1049), mirroring RedisPresenceRegistry.b64(); add a '|'-in-userId cross-node parse test.
backlog.untested: ClusterPresenceTest uses simple userIds only; no test exercises a '|'-containing userId across the parse path.

synthesis.summary: RC3 presence impl-review: 1 confirmed finding, verified real against the live code. ClusterMessageSender.firePresenceTransition (L1020) builds the cross-node presence-event body as raw `userId|old|new` and onPresenceMessage (L1040/1049) parses it with split("\\|",3) + PresenceStatus.valueOf; a userId containing '|' (composite/multi-tenant principal, SAML NameID) passes the length guard, throws valueOf on the third field, and is silently dropped on every REMOTE node — asymmetrically (origin fires the listener directly at L1013 with enum objects, no parsing), and the crash-path reap OFFLINE is among the lost events. Calibrated MAJOR (silent cross-node correctness loss, conditional on '|' in userId, not all traffic). DECISION: rc3ReadyToCut = TRUE. This MAJOR does NOT break any of the 3 design BLOCKER fixes (crash-path reap machinery + OFFLINE emission intact; reserved PRESENCE_CHANNEL with no version bump intact; OnAnyRedisSpiRequired untouched) nor the byte-identical-when-off contract (entire publish path gated behind presencePublishChanges / presence beans, dormant when off). Per the stated rule it goes to the RC3 backlog and the cut proceeds. Recommended pre-GA (or fast-follow) fix: base64url-encode parts[0] on publish and decode on receive, mirroring the storage-side RedisPresenceRegistry.b64() convention, plus a '|'-in-userId regression test (current ClusterPresenceTest uses simple userIds only).
